meaning: vt.棰勫厛瀹夋帓,浣垮亸鍚戜簬
examples: Stress can predispose people to heart attacks.
They take pains to hire people whose personalities predispose them to serve customers well.
And the trauma may, in fact, predispose people to having mental illness.
Birth complications also predispose to adult violence, especially when combined with negative home environments.
Their preliminary evidence suggests a genetic profile that may predispose a response to the drug.
